title: ANALYTICAL CHEMISTRY OF ZIRCONIUM. PART II. CINNAMIC ACID
author: CH. VENKATESWARLU
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Zirconium is shown to be precipitated quantitatively by cinnamic acid in 0.1  N  acid, HCl or HNO 3 . The metal up to o.6 mg. can be estimated and separated from thorium, manganese, uranium, cerite earths; beryllium, aluminium, nickel and iron with the reagent. Fe +++  atone requires a second preci­pitation.
steps_raw: Zirconium is shown to be precipitated quantitatively by cinnamic acid in 0.1  N  acid, HCl or HNO 3 . The metal up to o.6 mg. can be estimated and separated from thorium, manganese, uranium, cerite earths; beryllium, aluminium, nickel and iron with the reagent.
